name: Vucub Caquix
description: Vucub Caquix, whose name translates to 'Seven Macaw', is a boastful and arrogant bird-demon from Mayan mythology, primarily featured in the Popol Vuh. He falsely proclaimed himself to be the sun and the moon, misleading the early humans with his vanity and greed. Possessing brilliant, reflective teeth made of precious metals and a piercing gaze, he believed himself to be the source of light and power. His sons, Zipacna and Cabracan, were equally destructive forces, shaking mountains and the earth. The hero twins, Hunahpu and Xbalanque, eventually targeted Vucub Caquix and his sons to restore order and humility to the world. They used cunning and trickery to defeat him, removing his precious teeth and causing him to lose his power and influence. His downfall marked a significant step in the creation of the true sun and the establishment of the current age of humanity.
subtitle: Arrogant Bird-Demon of the Popol Vuh

keyMyths.title: Vucub Caquix's Claim
keyMyths.description: Vucub Caquix declared himself the sun and moon, leading to confusion and chaos amongst the early humans. His false light and boastful nature misled many and disrupted the natural order.

keyMyths.title: The Hero Twins' Plan
keyMyths.description: Hunahpu and Xbalanque recognized Vucub Caquix as a false deity and devised a plan to bring him down. They aimed to restore true light and order to the world.

keyMyths.title: The Downfall of Vucub Caquix
keyMyths.description: The hero twins tricked Vucub Caquix into eating poisoned food and removed his precious teeth. This act stripped him of his power and marked his ultimate defeat.

sources.author: Dennis Tedlock

sources.text: A modern translation and interpretation of the Popol Vuh.
